helpRoutes.purpose: Find the official recognition, reclaim and tax-return route rather than asking a charity regulator to decide tax status.
helpRoutes.firstStep: Identify whether the issue is regulator registration, HMRC recognition, a specific relief claim or a tax return; use the matching official service.
askFor: Which current application or filing route applies to the charity's legal form.
Which documents and HMRC reference are required.
How to correct a recognition or claim record through the official process.
safeContactRule: Do not send bank, tax-reference or official identity details to TaxSorted.
Use authenticated HMRC services for confidential matters.
Regulator registration is not a substitute for HMRC recognition.

helpRoutes.name: Ask the local council about charitable rate relief

evidence.locator: How to get relief and report changes

helpRoutes.purpose: Reach the billing authority that can check property use, mandatory charitable relief and any local discretionary top-up.
helpRoutes.firstStep: Find the council for the property and have the rates account, occupation, charity status and use evidence ready.
askFor: Whether the property and current use meet mandatory-relief conditions.
What evidence and application form the council needs.
Whether a published discretionary-relief policy applies and how to report changes.
safeContactRule: This route covers England only and is subject to the current private-school exclusion and full rules.
Do not assume registered charity status makes every property eligible.
Use the council's secure route for account-specific information.

helpRoutes.name: Check a government grant claim against official award data

evidence.locator: Annual grant award data and quality notes

helpRoutes.purpose: Test whether a stated government grant appears in the relevant official release without overstating what an award record proves.
helpRoutes.firstStep: Choose the release covering the award period, then search by exact award, company or charity number where available.
askFor: The exact award identifier and official source URL.
The funder, period, stated value and recipient identifier.
Separate evidence for payment, spending or impact if those are claimed.
safeContactRule: Do not publish a name-only match.
An absent result may reflect release scope, lag or a missing identifier.
Award does not equal payment, spending, delivery or impact.

helpRoutes.name: Check a public contract claim against official notice data

evidence.locator: Official notice services, OCDS records and supplier identifiers

helpRoutes.purpose: Test whether a stated public contract appears in the relevant official notice service without treating the notice as proof of delivery or payment.
helpRoutes.firstStep: Use the official page to identify the relevant notice service, then search by exact notice, OCID, company or charity number and covered period.
askFor: The exact notice or OCID and official source URL.
The buyer, period, stated value and supplier identifier.
Separate evidence for payment, delivery or impact if those are claimed.
safeContactRule: Do not publish a name-only supplier match.
An absent result may reflect notice scope, publication lag or a missing identifier.
Award notice does not equal payment, profit, delivery or impact.

pipelineStages.name: Write the purpose and forbidden uses first

evidence.locator: Own lawful basis, fairness, transparency and reasonable expectations
Belief data and deliberate inference boundary

pipelineStages.trigger: A proposal to add a new charity, finance, funding, premises or people source.
pipelineStages.action: Write the concrete public purpose, users, admitted fields, excluded fields, retention, correction route and emergency stop before collecting anything.
checks: Organisation transparency is the purpose; marketing, people profiling and belief inference are forbidden.
The same purpose cannot be achieved with less data.
The proposal has a named owner and deletion path.
pipelineStages.output: A versioned admission record or a documented refusal; no data is fetched at this stage.

pipelineStages.name: Check source authority, terms and reuse rights

evidence.locator: Official daily extract and table documentation
OGL attribution, no direct marketing and no false-register claim
Documented CSV boundary without stable bulk-feed terms
Reuser responsibility for data protection, copyright and other law
Official release licence, period and quality information
Official OCDS publication routes and page freshness

pipelineStages.action: Record the official publisher, authority, access route, licence, attribution, prohibited uses, freshness promise and fields actually covered.
checks: Copyright and database rights permit the proposed reuse.
Source-specific terms such as no direct marketing are enforceable in the product design.
Open licensing is not treated as permission to process personal data.
pipelineStages.output: A source-rights decision with the exact terms and review date.

pipelineStages.name: Assess personal and special-category data before collection

evidence.locator: Article 6 and 9, manifestly public, fairness and transparency
Religious-belief inferences are special-category processing
Filed documents can contain unnecessary personal material

pipelineStages.action: Classify each candidate field as organisation data, personal data, possible home or personal contact, special-category data or an inference risk.
checks: V1 has no people, named pay, personal contact, biography, donor or beneficiary fields.
Organisation religion-purpose fields never become a person's belief label.
Filed PDFs and free text are link-only unless a later field-specific review admits them.
pipelineStages.output: A field allowlist and quarantine list; uncertainty goes to quarantine rather than publication.

pipelineStages.name: Fetch only the documented official snapshot

evidence.locator: Documented full-register downloads
Official download and API application route
Browser CSV export rather than stable unattended feed
Documented company and accounts bulk products
Official annual grant award download
Official OCDS API and dated output routes

pipelineStages.trigger: A source and field allowlist have passed admission.
pipelineStages.action: Fetch from the documented official bulk or API route with a bounded retry count and no scraping fallback.
checks: The endpoint, terms and expected content match the admission record.
Authentication and rate limits are respected.
An unavailable or changed feed stops the run.
pipelineStages.output: An encrypted, access-restricted temporary raw snapshot plus fetch metadata, or no snapshot on failure. Persist its hash and manifest and the later sanitised allowlisted snapshot; securely delete prohibited raw bytes on the documented bounded schedule.

pipelineStages.name: Verify freshness, completeness and integrity

evidence.locator: Daily extract and data-definition tables
Daily register claim and five-year return data
On-demand CSV export and no stable unattended cadence
Monthly company and daily/monthly accounts product schedules
Annual release period and quality information
Notice API identifiers and publication dates

pipelineStages.action: Hash the bytes, record the publisher date and covered period, compare expected files and fields, and reject silent truncation or stale replacement.
checks: All expected tables or sheets are present and parseable.
The covered period is explicit and not represented as live.
Large count changes and schema drift stop automatic publication.
pipelineStages.output: A verified snapshot manifest with hash, period, schema fingerprint and counts.

pipelineStages.name: Project only admitted organisation fields

evidence.locator: Separate organisation, return, finance and trustee tables
Commission-restricted organisation CSV
Scottish register organisation, finance and public-contact fields
Company, accounts and PSC products as separate field families
Scheme and award field families with quality notes
Notice, buyer, supplier and optional identifier fields
Fairness, transparency and own-controller duties

pipelineStages.trigger: The snapshot manifest passes integrity checks.
pipelineStages.action: Select only stable organisation identity, status, official purpose classification, legal form, finance totals, exact organisation relationships and safe generic help links.
checks: Unknown new fields are rejected rather than silently copied.
Addresses, emails and phones need a separate home/personal-contact admission decision.
Trustee, officer, donor, beneficiary and free-text people fields are excluded.
pipelineStages.output: A typed organisation-only staging table and a rejected-field report.

pipelineStages.name: Quarantine people, contacts and belief risk

evidence.locator: Manifestly public and controller-duty limits
Special-category inference regardless of correctness
Accounts published in the form received
Public organisation contact fields and sensitive-data claim
Registered-office and PSC personal-data risk with reuser responsibility
Recipient fields and release quality context
Supplier and notice contact fields

pipelineStages.action: Apply a deterministic schema allowlist and validate every value in every admitted contact-like field for people, personal or residential contacts, signatures, named pay and belief-inference risk; reject or quarantine free text by default, using samples only as additional quality assurance.
checks: Organisation religion-purpose classifications remain organisation-level and source-asserted.
No name search, person graph or cross-register people join can be built.
Quarantined data has restricted access and a deletion schedule rather than a hidden public endpoint.
pipelineStages.output: A cleared organisation-only dataset and a private quarantine audit with no public values.

pipelineStages.name: Join only on exact official organisation identifiers

evidence.locator: Company-number keyed company and accounts products
Grant award recipient fields and quality notes
Optional supplier charity and company identifiers

pipelineStages.trigger: Cleared organisation tables and admitted external funding or company tables are available.
pipelineStages.action: Resolve regulator and company identifiers in their documented namespaces; names are display text and never join keys.
checks: Company joins use an exact filed company number.
Grant and contract joins require an exact published charity or company number.
Name-similar and ambiguous candidates remain unpublished.
pipelineStages.output: Exact organisation relationships with source identifiers, or an unresolved record with no public relationship.

pipelineStages.name: Normalise finance without flattening meaning

evidence.locator: Income bands and filed-account requirements
Receipts-and-payments versus fully accrued accounts
Income and legal-form dependent accounts
Award data period and quality caveats
Award and contract notice stages

pipelineStages.action: Store currency, amount, period, accounting basis, entity or group scope, restriction and source category alongside each organisation-level figure.
checks: Income is not labelled profit, cash or unrestricted funds.
Award is not labelled payment, spend, output or impact.
Employee bands and trustee-remuneration indicators remain aggregate and unnamed.
pipelineStages.output: Comparable fields only where definitions align, with original labels and caveats retained everywhere else.

pipelineStages.name: Attach evidence to every published field

evidence.locator: Separate documented source tables
Attribution and derivative-register condition
Register export fields and coverage context

pipelineStages.trigger: Normalised organisation and finance records exist.
pipelineStages.action: Attach source ID, official identifier, snapshot hash, observed date, original field, transformation method and direct official URL to each record.
checks: Every value can be traced without a name search.
Self-reported fields are labelled as such.
Analysis is distinguished from official law, policy, register data and guidance.
pipelineStages.output: A provenance-complete candidate release and machine-readable source manifest.

pipelineStages.name: Fail closed at the publication gate

evidence.locator: Reuse terms and data availability
No supported unattended feed established
Controller accountability and public-domain limits

pipelineStages.trigger: A provenance-complete candidate release is ready.
pipelineStages.action: Run schema, reference, licence, privacy, freshness, count-drift and unsafe-field checks; stop by default until a human explicitly approves unchanged or reviewed terms and fields.
checks: Any missing source, uncertain right, personal field, belief inference or schema drift stops publication.
The emergency stop overrides every enable switch.
Closed data still exposes source, register, gap, schema and release metadata so the boundary is transparent.
pipelineStages.output: A recorded publish decision after explicit approval, or a stopped release with public reason metadata and no organisation rows.

pipelineStages.name: Correct, expire and tombstone without rewriting history

evidence.locator: Status, event history and date fields
Former charities and latest changes
Register status and annual-reporting fields
Company status and previous names
Versioned annual award release and quality information
Notice identifiers, stages and publication dates

pipelineStages.trigger: An official source changes, a correction is accepted, or an organisation is removed, dissolved or replaced.
pipelineStages.action: Publish the corrected current version, retain the evidence-backed snapshot history, and tombstone stable TaxSorted IDs rather than reassigning them.
checks: Removal and dissolution dates come from exact official identifiers.
A correction never silently erases the source and observation date of the earlier value.
Urgent safety removals can hide a value immediately while review continues.
pipelineStages.output: A current record, version history and non-reused tombstone with the correction reason.

pipelineStages.name: Start help discovery at the official register

evidence.locator: Official organisation identity, activities and public-contact fields
Scottish register search and terms
Northern Ireland search and coverage boundary

pipelineStages.trigger: A person wants to find an organisation that may offer relevant help.
pipelineStages.action: Choose the correct territorial register and verify official number, status, purposes, activities, geography and own website.
checks: Absence can reflect excepted, exempt, unregistered or incomplete-register coverage.
A purpose classification does not guarantee current service capacity.
No person, trustee or belief search is performed.
pipelineStages.output: A shortlist of official organisation pages chosen by the user, not a TaxSorted-ranked people or religion profile.

pipelineStages.name: Move to the organisation's current generic service page

evidence.locator: Public website and organisation contact fields
Public website/contact fields and no-direct-marketing term
Public contact fields in register search/export

pipelineStages.trigger: The user selects an official organisation entry.
pipelineStages.action: Follow the organisation's own current website to its generic service, referral or contact route and read eligibility and safeguarding instructions.
checks: The destination belongs to the organisation and uses HTTPS.
A register postal address is not used if it may be residential.
No extracted personal email, phone or trustee contact is offered.
pipelineStages.output: The organisation's current generic help route with the date checked and official register backlink.

pipelineStages.name: Make one specific, proportionate request

evidence.locator: Self-reported activities, beneficiaries and public contact
No-direct-marketing restriction
Organisation activities and public-contact fields

pipelineStages.trigger: The user has read the organisation's current help process.
pipelineStages.action: Ask whether the service covers the need, geography and eligibility; request required documents, accessibility, costs, timing and alternatives if it cannot help.
checks: Send one relevant message rather than bulk outreach.
Share only information required by the published process.
Do not represent a historic project, purpose label or grant as a promise of help.
pipelineStages.output: A user-controlled request to the organisation, with no TaxSorted storage of its contents.

transparencyGaps.name: No organisation directory in v1

evidence.locator: National-scale source tables exceed this bounded system map
Reuse restrictions and unavailable feed observation
Browser export and incomplete-register context

transparencyGaps.title: V1 intentionally contains no charity organisation records
transparencyGaps.detail: A complete national mirror needs partitioned storage, source-specific terms, correction handling and a field-level privacy assessment; hand-picking prominent charities would create an arbitrary and misleading sample.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Use the three official register help routes to answer who exists. TaxSorted v1 answers how the system works and publishes no organisation row, ranking or curated example.

transparencyGaps.name: People and belief profiling excluded

evidence.locator: Article 9 and manifestly-public conditions
Religious-belief data and deliberate inference

transparencyGaps.title: Public trustee names do not justify a people or religion graph
transparencyGaps.detail: Trustee, officer, biography, work-history, named-pay, portrait, donor, beneficiary and personal-contact processing can create personal and special-category risk, particularly when association is used to infer religious belief.
transparencyGaps.consequence: V1 has no people collection, name search, fuzzy person join or inferred denomination. A later proposal would need a separate necessity, Article 6, Article 9, transparency and rights assessment before collection.

transparencyGaps.name: No single complete UK charity register

evidence.locator: England and Wales register scope
Scottish Charity Register scope
Northern Ireland register and coverage warning

transparencyGaps.title: Three territorial registers have different tests, fields and gaps
transparencyGaps.detail: England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland have separate regulators and register products; legal status, identifiers, update cadence, accounting rules and public fields are not interchangeable.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Every organisation query needs a jurisdiction and source namespace. A UK-wide search must preserve territorial meaning and cross-border duplicates rather than pretending one row is authoritative everywhere.

transparencyGaps.name: England and Wales register omissions

evidence.locator: Excepted, exempt and below-threshold charities
TaxSorted consequence of the registration coverage boundary

transparencyGaps.title: Absence from the England and Wales register is not conclusive
transparencyGaps.detail: Below-threshold charities and lawful excepted or exempt charities can be outside the ordinary registered-charity dataset. Qualifying linked church charities at or below the stated income boundary have a time-limited exception currently ending 31 March 2031.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Do not label an absent organisation non-charitable or unregulated without checking current registration, exception and exemption rules.

transparencyGaps.name: Northern Ireland coverage and feed gap

evidence.locator: Coverage context and on-demand CSV export
TaxSorted response to incomplete coverage and unsupported automation

transparencyGaps.title: The Northern Ireland register is not the full sector and lacks a confirmed unattended feed
transparencyGaps.detail: CCNI explains that current registration and Expression of Intent context limits completeness; the reviewed guide documents browser CSV export but not a stable supported bulk endpoint or update service level.
transparencyGaps.consequence: TaxSorted remains manual-only and has no Northern Ireland charity-by-charity subject rows. It will not scrape around the official export or treat absence as proof of status.

transparencyGaps.name: OSCR feed availability

evidence.locator: Official download page present; linked full-download retrieval failed during the 2026-07-10 review
TaxSorted fail-closed response to the observed availability gap

transparencyGaps.title: The OSCR download promise and observed endpoint availability did not align
transparencyGaps.detail: The official page described a daily full-register download, but the linked artifact was not reliably retrievable during review. This may be temporary or a link change; TaxSorted does not know which.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Collection fails closed and reports source availability rather than substituting scraping or a stale third-party copy.

transparencyGaps.name: Selected legal-form coverage

evidence.locator: England and Wales common forms
Scottish SCIO and charitable-company comparison
Northern Ireland reporting varies by legal structure

transparencyGaps.title: Legal-form records are selected and jurisdiction-specific, not a complete UK catalogue
transparencyGaps.detail: V1 models common England and Wales forms and the Scottish SCIO, plus a Scottish charitable-company reporting link. It does not yet publish jurisdiction-specific Northern Ireland company, trust or association records, nor every statutory, chartered, community-benefit or cross-border form.
transparencyGaps.consequence: The legalFormIds on control models are illustrative selected links, not an exhaustive statement that a control pattern cannot exist in another lawful form or nation.

transparencyGaps.name: HMRC recognition is not a public charity register field

evidence.locator: Separate HMRC recognition application
Recognition required to benefit from relief

transparencyGaps.title: Regulator registration does not publicly prove HMRC tax recognition
transparencyGaps.detail: HMRC recognition is a separate confidential administrative relationship. The charity registers can show regulator status and some Gift Aid indicators but not the complete HMRC tax account or every relief decision.
transparencyGaps.consequence: APIs must not expose a derived boolean claiming current HMRC recognition unless HMRC provides an authoritative public field for that exact purpose.

transparencyGaps.name: Named pay and work history are unavailable by design

evidence.locator: Aggregate employee benefit bands and trustee-payment indicators
Fairness and reasonable expectations in public-domain reuse

transparencyGaps.title: Official structured sources do not provide a complete, comparable named-pay or career dataset
transparencyGaps.detail: Registers can expose aggregate employee bands, trustee-payment indicators and filed notes. They do not provide a complete official CV, prior-employer history or named salary series for everyone involved. Any reuse of fragmentary public personal data requires a separate purpose, necessity, basis, fairness and rights assessment.
transparencyGaps.consequence: V1 publishes disclosure definitions only. A later organisation layer may publish admitted organisation-level aggregate bands after source, rights and privacy review; it must not add biography enrichment, social-profile matching or named compensation estimates.

transparencyGaps.name: Accounts are not a live asset inventory

evidence.locator: Filed accounts by income and form
Receipts-and-payments and fully accrued accounts
Accounting form varies by income and legal structure

transparencyGaps.title: Asset totals vary by accounting basis, period, fund and group scope
transparencyGaps.detail: Filed accounts are periodic financial statements, not a live property, bank-account or investment inventory. Small-charity receipts-and-payments accounts and full accrual accounts are not directly equivalent.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Any future API must retain period, basis, valuation, restricted-fund and charity-versus-group labels and must not claim a complete current asset list.

transparencyGaps.name: Public organisation contacts can still be personal

evidence.locator: Public address, email and telephone fields in export
Unnecessary personal material in filed documents
Public-domain information and controller obligations

transparencyGaps.title: A register contact field may be a person's home, email or telephone
transparencyGaps.detail: A field published as the charity's contact can still identify an individual or reveal a residential address, especially for a small organisation.
transparencyGaps.consequence: V1 links to official register and organisation help pages but publishes no contact values. A future contact field needs home-address detection, necessity and an organisation-versus-person assessment.

transparencyGaps.name: Funding identifiers are incomplete

evidence.locator: Award data scope and quality notes
Supplier charity or company numbers only where supplied
TaxSorted exact-identifier publication rule

transparencyGaps.title: Many grant and contract rows cannot be safely joined to a charity
transparencyGaps.detail: Recipient and supplier identifiers are inconsistent or optional, and published names can be ambiguous, changed or shared.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Only exact official identifier joins may publish. Unmatched and fuzzy candidates remain unresolved and are never counted as confirmed funding.

transparencyGaps.name: Filed documents mix transparency and personal material

evidence.locator: Accounts published in the format received without regulator redaction
Reuser copyright and data-protection responsibility
Public-domain information does not remove controller duties
TaxSorted link-only and no-free-text extraction boundary

transparencyGaps.title: Public PDF access is not a blanket extraction licence
transparencyGaps.detail: Accounts and reports can contain signatures, incidental names, images, narrative and other personal or copyrighted material even when the document is lawfully public at the source.
transparencyGaps.consequence: TaxSorted links to documents and extracts no people or free text in v1. Structured extraction needs a separate field, copyright and data-protection review.

transparencyGaps.name: England and Wales accounting transition

evidence.locator: Expected threshold changes from 30 September 2026 and SORP 2026
Current bands on review date
TaxSorted transition and client-review warning

transparencyGaps.title: Current and expected future accounting thresholds must not be mixed
transparencyGaps.detail: The regulator describes announced accounting and examination threshold changes expected for accounting years ending on or after 30 September 2026, while current guidance still applies before the effective transition; SORP 2026 has its own period start rule.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Every threshold answer needs the accounting period and current rule source. The corpus should be refreshed around the transition, and clients should check each source's reviewAfter date; this record does not claim an automatic date-based stop exists.

transparencyGaps.name: Public-benefit analysis is territorially scoped

evidence.locator: England and Wales public-benefit framework
UK charity tax charitable-purpose condition

transparencyGaps.title: The published public-benefit bargain analysis is scoped to England and Wales
transparencyGaps.detail: HMRC tax rules are UK-wide, but charity tests and public-benefit law differ territorially. V1's explicit bargain analysis uses the England and Wales Charity Commission framework and must not be silently projected onto Scotland or Northern Ireland.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Add separately sourced OSCR and CCNI analysis before presenting an equivalent territorial rationale as established for those jurisdictions.

transparencyGaps.name: Field-level territorial evidence remains a manual review

evidence.locator: Territorial source schema
Territorial source schema and terms
Territorial export schema and coverage

transparencyGaps.title: Source-union coverage cannot prove every sentence applies across every declared territory
transparencyGaps.detail: The loader can reject a UK record whose cited sources collectively omit a nation, but only human field-level review can tell whether each individual claim is supported for every territory listed.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Release review must inspect field evidence and narrow jurisdictions where support is territorial; passing the automated union check is necessary but not sufficient.
